---
title: "TensorCAR"
---

## Overview

Phase 2.1 of the [multi-channel storage roadmap](../DataObjects/multi_channel_storage_and_transforms.qmd).
Applies **Common Average Reference (CAR)** to a multi-channel `TensorData`.
For each time point, the transform subtracts a cross-channel reference signal
(mean or median) from every channel:

$$\text{result}[t, c] = \text{input}[t, c] - r(t)$$

where $r(t)$ is the mean or median across all non-excluded channels at time $t$.
This removes common-mode noise shared across channels — a standard preprocessing
step in extracellular electrophysiology.

## Location

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorCAR/TensorCAR.hpp` | Header: `TensorCARParams`, `CARMethod`, `tensorCAR()` |
| `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorCAR/TensorCAR.cpp` | Implementation |
| `src/TransformsV2/algorithms/TensorCAR/TensorCAR.test.cpp` | Catch2 tests |

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `method` | `CARMethod` | `Median` | `Mean` or `Median` reference computation |
| `exclude_channels` | `std::vector<int>` | `{}` | 0-based channel indices excluded from reference computation (still receive the reference subtraction) |
| `use_gpu` | `bool` | `false` | Reserved for future LibTorch GPU path; currently unused |

## Compute Backend

The current implementation uses **Armadillo** for computation:

1. Retrieve the underlying `arma::fmat` via `input.asArmadilloMatrix()`.
   Shape: `(num_samples, num_channels)` — column-major.
2. If `exclude_channels` is non-empty, restrict reference computation to
   the complement sub-matrix via `mat.cols(include_uvec)`.
3. Compute reference column vector (one value per time point):
   - Mean: `arma::mean(ref_source, 1)` → `(num_samples, 1)`
   - Median: `arma::median(ref_source, 1)` → `(num_samples, 1)`
4. Subtract: `result_mat.each_col() -= ref_col`
   — subtracts `ref[t]` from every channel at every time point.
5. Reconstruct a `TensorData` preserving the input's `RowDescriptor`
   (TimeFrameIndex, Interval, or Ordinal) and column names.

### Future GPU Path

The `use_gpu` flag is reserved for a future LibTorch implementation following
the roadmap algorithm:

```cpp
auto t = tensor.toLibTorch();
if (use_gpu) { t = DeviceManager::instance().toDevice(t); }
auto ref = torch::median(t, /*dim=*/1).values;
t = t - ref.unsqueeze(1);
// return TensorData wrapping LibTorchTensorStorage
```

## Row Descriptor Preservation

The output has exactly the same shape and `RowDescriptor` as the input:

| Input `RowType` | Factory used |
|-----------------|-------------|
| `TimeFrameIndex` | `TensorData::createTimeSeries2D(...)` |
| `Interval` | `TensorData::createFromIntervals(...)` |
| `Ordinal` | `TensorData::createFromArmadillo(...)` |

## Registration

- **Registry name:** `"TensorCAR"`
- **Category:** Signal Processing
- **Transform type:** Container (`TensorData` → `TensorData`)
- Registered in `src/TransformsV2/core/RegisteredTransforms.cpp`

## Tests

The test file verifies:

| Test | What is checked |
|------|----------------|
| Registration | `ElementRegistry::isContainerTransform("TensorCAR")` |
| Mean CAR | Row-wise mean ≈ 0 after transform |
| Median CAR | Row-wise median ≈ 0 after transform |
| Default params | Median is default |
| Excluded channels | Reference computed from non-excluded channels only; excluded channels still receive the reference |
| Shape/name preservation | Same rows, same columns, same column names |
| RowDescriptor: TimeFrameIndex | Preserved with same `TimeFrame` pointer |
| RowDescriptor: Ordinal | Preserved |
| RowDescriptor: Interval | Preserved |
| Via ElementRegistry | Full execution path |
| 1D input | Returns `nullptr` |
| All channels excluded | Returns `nullptr` |
| Single channel | Output is all zeros |
